zh.Cambron-Casteau, Hainaut, Belgium: 比利時海瑙特坎布洛-卡斯奧
zh.lifeSummary: Jean II Guillaume Barbançon是Jean Ier de Barbançon和Marie d'Antoing的兒子，於1282年出生在法國。他於1314年之前與Eustachie alias Isabeau de Dargies結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1332年10月5日享年50歲。

zh-hans.Cambron-Casteau, Hainaut, Belgium: 比利时海瑙特坎布洛-卡斯奧
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jean II Guillaume Barbançon是Jean Ier de Barbançon和Marie d'Antoing的儿子，于1282年出生在法国。他于1314年之前与Eustachie alias Isabeau de Dargies结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1332年10月5日享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: Jean II Guillaume Barbançon was born in 1282, in France as the son of Jean Ier de Barbançon and Marie d'Antoing. He married Eustachie alias Isabeau de Dargies before 1314. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 5 October 1332, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Jean II Guillaume Barbançon est né en 1282, à France, fils de Jean Ier de Barbançon et Marie d'Antoing. Il a épousé Eustachie alias Isabeau de Dargies avant 1314. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 5 octobre 1332, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jean II Guillaume Barbançon wurde 1282 in Frankreich, als Sohn von Jean Ier de Barbançon und Marie d'Antoing geboren. Er hat vor 1314 Eustachie alias Isabeau de Dargies geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 5. Oktober 1332 im Alter von 50 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Jean II Guillaume Barbançon è nato il nel 1282 nel Francia come figlio di Jean Ier de Barbançon e Marie d'Antoing. Lui sposato Eustachie alias Isabeau de Dargies prima del 1314. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 5 ottobre 1332, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.Cambron-Casteau, Hainaut, Belgium: ベルギーヘイナトカンブロン-カステアウ
ja.lifeSummary: Jean II Guillaume Barbançonは1282年にフランスでJean Ier de BarbançonとMarie d'Antoingの息子として生まれた。1314年以前にEustachie alias Isabeau de Dargiesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1332年10月5日に50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean II Guillaume Barbançon(은)는 1282년 프랑스에서 Jean Ier de Barbançon(와)과 Marie d'Antoing의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1314년 이전 Eustachie alias Isabeau de Dargies(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1332년 10월 5일 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jean II Guillaume Barbançon nasceu em 1282, em França filho de Jean Ier de Barbançon e Marie d'Antoing. Ele casou-se com Eustachie alias Isabeau de Dargies antes 1314. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 5 de outubro de 1332, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jean II Guillaume Barbançon родился в 1282 г. в местности Франция. Его родителей звали Jean Ier de Barbançon и Marie d'Antoing. Он женился на женщине по имени Eustachie alias Isabeau de Dargies перед 1314 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 5 октября 1332 г. в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Jean II Guillaume Barbançon nació en 1282, en Francia como hijo de Jean Ier de Barbançon y Marie d'Antoing. Se casó con Eustachie alias Isabeau de Dargies antes de 1314. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 5 de octubre de 1332, a la edad de 50 años.
